docs.title: AI in Biological Sciences
docs.text:
Welcome to the documentation for the AI in Biological Sciences textbook companion repository.

This site provides:

  • Chapter-by-chapter reference pages
  • API documentation for all biological AI endpoints
  • Interactive tutorials
  • Worked examples in Python, R, and Julia

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/DaScient/ai-in-biology.git
cd ai-in-biology
pip install -e ".[dev,docs]"

See the Quick Start tutorial to run your first analysis.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:
• :material-dna:{ .lg .middle } Protein Structure Prediction

    Predict 3D structure from sequence with the /protein/fold endpoint.

    :octicons-arrow-right-24: Tutorial

  • :material-chart-scatter-plot:{ .lg .middle } Genomics Analysis

    Variant-effect prediction and sequence modeling.

    :octicons-arrow-right-24: Tutorial

  • :material-microscope:{ .lg .middle } Single-Cell Intelligence

    Embed cells, infer trajectories, annotate types.

    :octicons-arrow-right-24: API

  • :material-leaf:{ .lg .middle } Ecology & SDM

    Species distribution modeling under environmental change.

    :octicons-arrow-right-24: API

docs.title: About
docs.text: This repository accompanies the textbook "AI in Biological Sciences: Theory, Applications, Practice, and Society" (DaScient Press, 2025) by Dr. Aris Thorne, Wei Chen, and Marcus Adebayo.

docs.title: API Overview
docs.text: The AI in Biological Sciences API exposes biological AI models via a RESTful HTTP interface.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text:
https://api.bioai.dascient.com

For local development:

http://localhost:8000

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: Currently no authentication is required for the development API. Production endpoints require a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

docs.title: Available Endpoint Groups
docs.text: Group Prefix Description Protein /api/v1/protein Structure prediction and design Genomics /api/v1/genomics Variant analysis and regulatory elements Single-Cell /api/v1/scell Embeddings and trajectory inference Ecology /api/v1/ecology Species distribution and tipping points Drug Discovery /api/v1/drug Molecular docking and generation

docs.title: Interactive Docs
docs.text: Visit /docs for the Swagger UI or /redoc for ReDoc when the API server is running.

docs.title: Protein Structure Prediction
docs.text:
Predict 3D protein structure from sequence using the /protein/fold endpoint.

Tutorial stub

This tutorial is under construction. For now, see the Quick Start and the API overview.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• A working installation of ai-bio-sciences (see Quick Start)
  • An API key, if calling the hosted API

docs.title: Next steps
docs.text: Watch this page for the full walk-through.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This tutorial walks you through installing the package and running your first API call.

docs.title: 1. Install
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/DaScient/ai-in-biology.git
cd ai-in-biology
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: 2. Start the API
docs.text:
make api-run
# or directly:
uvicorn api.src.main:app --reload

docs.title: 3. Health Check
docs.text:
curl http://localhost:8000/health
# {"status":"healthy","version":"0.1.0"}

docs.title: 4. Predict Protein Structure
docs.text:
import httpx

response = httpx.post(
    "http://localhost:8000/api/v1/protein/fold",
    json={"sequence": "MKTIIALSYIFCLVFA"},
)
print(response.json())

docs.title: 5. Open Interactive Docs
docs.text: Navigate to http://localhost:8000/docs to explore all endpoints.
